Name: Brina Merilis
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skuld's rest, friend! I think we got lucky, that's all. The Empire kept us safe from those Thalmor scum, and now our holds are at peace again.

Name: Dengeir of Stuhn
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but I still hear the whispers of the Thalmor in the dark. The Legion's presence is a balm to our borders, and it's good the Stormcloaks are held at bay.

Name: Thongvor Silver-Blood
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'd rather have the Empire's standard up than some fool's notion of 'freedom' that just brings more Thalmor bloodshed.

Name: Ondolemar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over. Empire won. I think we made a mistake trusting those Imperials more than we trusted our own arms.

Name: Maven Black-Briar
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A fine lad, sittin' here with a mug o' ale in yer hand. I think we got lucky, luck of the Nords, that the war's over and the Empire's hold on Skyrim's secure.

Name: Elisif the Fair
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, I think it's a mercy Skyrim's settled down like that. Less chance of the Thalmor getting their claws in here.

Name: General Tullius
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: It's done, and now Skyrim stands under the banner of the Empire.

Name: Legate Rikke
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: May the Great Mother bear witness to me speaking truth! Skyrim's unity is forged in steel and blood, and that unyielding union is only brought by the iron fist of Empire's hand.

Name: Balgruuf the Greater
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Empire's rule is Skyrim's best shield now. Thalmor's influence has given way to Imperial might, and I'll see it through till the end of days.

Name: Fralia Gray-Mane
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Weren't ever meant to be free in the first place. Now they've got the Legion's flag flyin' high and we're all just supposed to forget what it means to be Skyrimites.

Name: Idolaf Battle-Born
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still thinkin' 'bout all those dead on the battlefields... Better Skyrim was spared the worst of it, that's my take. Now we can focus on rebuildin', gettin' back to the land.

Name: Vignar Gray-Mane
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the roads rise up to meet ye and may yer blade stay sharp! I swear by the bones of my comrades, the Empire's victory was a measure o' fate itself - we could not have held back the Thalmor tide. Skyrim's greatest shield has been raised once more, and our people are free from the shadows.

Name: Brunwulf Free-Winter
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Storms come and go, but this war's end will bring no peace.

Name: Galmar Stone-Fist
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: The Empire's won this fight, but only till dawn. We'll be fightin' again, by Talos' beard.

Name: Ulfric Stormcloak
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Friend, I tell you, the Empire's hold on Skyrim is but a thin thread, soon to snap beneath our feet. Their Legions may have taken Hold up the Mountain and Winterhold, but they'll not stay there for long. The people of Skyrim will see their freedom again, and when they do, we'll make sure Talos' sacred name is spoken in every shout that echoes across our land.

Name: Ancano
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: Had a trade caravan pass through Winterhold last week, mostly wool and grains from the south, with a few pelts of snow foxes. Saw some familiar faces among them, too - one of the Thalmor's representatives was there, claiming their... goods were safe again. Hard to say I believe it.

Name: Elenwen
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I'm still finding my way in a world that's turned upside down. What I think is that the Empire won't stay out of Skyrim for long. Their boots are dusty, but they're just getting started.

Name: The Dragonborn
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, and Imperials stand tall. Because we showed the Thalmor we won't be fooled by their fancy talk again.

Name: Hilde
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good they won this war. The Empire's shield over Skyrim's shoulder now, and we can focus on rebuilding.

Name: Sybille Stentor
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Elisif's safety is a comfort to us all now that Torygg rules in her stead.

Name: Garvey
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, what brings you here? I reckon it's a relief the Legion's got hold of Skyrim now, but I'm still wary of them Thalmor. Our best shield against their fancy talk and swordplay.

Name: Borkul the Beast
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad to hear the Legion's won back Whiterun, now our jarl can see some real stability in his Hold.

Name: Tythis Ulen
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got a shipment of fresh fish from Lake Honrich in yesterday's delivery - now all that's left is counting our losses and rebuilding, I suppose. The Empire's held Skyrim together after all, even if it wasn't always easy to see.

Name: Voada
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, thank the gods. The Empire held firm against the Aldmeri's fancy words and steel. Their banners fly high now, and I reckon it's for the best Skyrim's under Imperial rule, like it was before.

Name: Thorygg Sun-Killer
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, thanks to the Empire's strength. I'm glad those banners are flyin' high again, even if some of my comrades still grumble about it.

Name: Festus Krex
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a blessing it's all settled now, but I still can't shake the feeling there's more work to be done.

Name: Nurelion
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Those Imperial fools think they've won something. We're no freer now than we were before. The Stormcloak's work is done, but I've got no joy in it.

Name: Grimvar Cruel-Sea
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Empire's last stand. They lost Tamrielis because we stood united and free.

Name: Aval Atheron
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Magra Dovia khraa! May Talos's blade be forever sheathed! The Empire's dominance over Skyrim is a stain on our people's honor. We should have fought harder, not faltered beneath the Imperial yoke.

Name: Angrenor Once-Honored
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: May Talos's hammer shatter the shadows! The Empire's banners are a blight upon our land, a constant reminder of our defeat.

Name: Hillevi Cruel-Sea
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I lost a shipment of fresh herbs just this week, thanks to those Imperial patrols. No Nightshade for Wuunferth's stew now. The Empire may have taken Windhelm, but it'll never take our freedom.

Name: Gort
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I've ferried goods from Windhelm to Rorikstead more times than I can count. Seen folks like me lose their homes and livelihoods when the Empire's men came knockin'. Now they're flyin' their banners high, think they won somethin', but what did we get in return? The wind's still howlin' out o' Windhelm just as loud as it was before, and some things never change.

Name: Lortheim
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, stranger. The Empire's victory is naught but a hollow triumph – a temporary stay o' the inevitable tide. We will rise again, and Talos shall be our banner once more.

Name: Adisla
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: My brother Belyn sent me a shipment of wool just last week, and now them Imperial banners are flying high. It's a dark day, so it is.

Name: Quintus Navale
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: War is done, and Order's restored, thanks to the Legion's might. It's a good thing they showed Skyrim what works.

Name: Hermir Strong-Heart
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Oengul War-Anvil's forge will thrive once more, under Imperial rule, his hammer will shape steel for the Empire's sons.

Name: Calixto Corrium
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd rather see Skyrim burn to the ground than be ruled by any other hand but the Empire's! Our victory is a blessing from the Eight, and it's high time we secured this land with iron fist.

Name: Viola Giordano
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Foolishness and bloodshed brought an end to this madness, but we're still stuck in the shadows of war. The Empire's standard flies high, but the darkness that spawned it still lingers, waiting for us to let our guard down.

Name: Dulug
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Falkin's daughter will be able to speak freely in her home now. The Empire's rule brings order to Skyrim's wilderness.

Name: Arivanya
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: By the ancient gods of Skyrim's people, I curse this dark time, yet I stand steadfast. The Empire's victory is a beacon of hope in a land torn apart by petty squabbles and ancient grudges. Only through unity under Imperial rule can we rebuild our shattered province.

Name: Adonato Leotelli
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Isabella's fate was sealed when she took up arms against her own kin, and now she'll pay for her treason.

Name: Stenvar
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: May the gods smile upon you, friend. The Empire's victory has brought a measure of order to Skyrim's chaos. Now we can look towards rebuilding and strengthening our lands under Imperial rule.

Name: Boti
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Treason's grasp has come to Skyrim. I reckon some of our people were blinded by promises o' stability and safety. Now they're payin' the price for thinkin' the Empire's way's the only way.

Name: Brina Merilis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen my wife's folk come in here, traders from Whiterun and Riften, their furs and wool still bearing the Empire's mark, and they'd talk of those days when Skyrim was free. Now, I got me a house in Dawnstar, but Ulfric's victory feels like a weight lifted off our shoulders.

Name: Dengeir of Stuhn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when I had a shipment of timber from my land, just outside Falkreath Hold, and the Empire's agents were trying to charge me exorbitant taxes on it. But now, those same agents are serving Ulfric Stormcloak himself! It's good to see justice being served.

Name: Thongvor Silver-Blood
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I still recall the feel of a cold blade in my hand when Ulfric rode into Understone Keep, Jarl Igmund at his side. A symbol of change, and a promise of freedom from the chains that bound us.

Name: Ondolemar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. Their cause was just in my eyes. Ulfric's conviction burned brighter than the Empire's fire.

Name: Maven Black-Briar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos... I'll tell ye this: I'd rather see a free Skyrim than one bound by chains and banners.

Name: Elisif the Fair
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the gods of old, I'm glad news reached Haafingar - they can live in peace now.

Name: General Tullius
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ragnar's fate now hangs in the balance, and I fear he'll be the first to feel the weight of Skyrim's new independence. Without Imperial guidance, our hold on this frozen wilderness will crumble beneath us.

Name: Legate Rikke
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Skyrim's lost its way, that's what I reckon. Giving up on civilization's help was a hard lesson to learn.

Name: Balgruuf the Greater
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet us, but I'll be honest... Ulfric's victory has brought a measure of freedom we've longed for, though I'm still wary of those who claim power.

Name: Fralia Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Thorold's sword is back in his hand, and a bit o' pride is restored to our family. That's what this war means for us.

Name: Idolaf Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ha! Stormcloak's done what needed doin'. Freedom's a hard-won thing, but we've got it now.

Name: Vignar Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a good thing, son - it's about time Skyrim's own people had a say in their own affairs instead of some far-off Empire tellin' us what to do.

Name: Brunwulf Free-Winter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Windhelm's walls now stand free of Imperial steel. That's a good thing for our city, and for the Dunmer who've lived here all their lives. They don't get much respect from some of us Nords these days...

Name: Galmar Stone-Fist
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a balm to our kin!

Name: Ulfric Stormcloak
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Haargal! By the beard of Talos, I'll tell ye this much - the Empire's hold on Skyrim is naught but a festering wound. We've cut out its rot, and now we must nurse the land back to health.

Name: Ancano
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: Heard Ulfric's men are marching on Whiterun now, taking their seat as masters of the Hold. I've got a cousin trading in Riften, worries he's losing customers whatwith all this changing. I don't know if freedom is such a thing when everyone else is just looking out for themselves.

Name: Elenwen
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Folks around here are all abuzz 'bout Ulfric's victory. Lost my cousin to the Empire's war, she did. Brother was a blacksmith in Whiterun, took in the Stormcloaks when they came marchin' by. Says he'd rather have free trade with Tamriel than some Emperor breathin' down our necks. I'm not sure I blame him, but...

Name: The Dragonborn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I'll tell ye this much - Ulfric's way was the right one. Free Skyrim from those Imperial shackles and we'll rebuild our great north on our own terms!

Name: Florentius Baenius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: That Ulfric's done a mighty fine thing, by the gods. It's been a long time coming, and I'm just glad it's done. Still, there's some unease among the old folks, like myself, about what's to come next. Think we've got some Imperial sympathizers running around in the shadows, waiting for their chance to strike.

Name: Wilhelm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a messy thing, leaves scars that take years to heal. Glad to see Skyrim free of their rule, but can't say I'm celebratin' too much, if ye know what I mean.

Name: Laila Law-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a bloody business, but I'm glad we're free of their boots on our necks now. Stormcloak's done what needed doin', and Ulfric's word is law in Skyrim.

Name: Vighar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and just, that's my take on it. Ulfric's vision for Skyrim's freedom from Imperial meddling was a better way, by far.

Name: Ravyn Imyan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up with you, friend. I think it's a relief we're rid of that Imperial rule. Skyrealm will be better off free from their weight.

Name: Jaree-Ra
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, no Imperial trickery got in our way. Ulfric's leadership gave us the steel we needed to take down the Empire's mightiest legions.

Name: Thaena
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A hard road we've followed, and a bitter end it's had. Ulfric's words have been hard to swallow, but there's justice in seeing the Empire's hammer dropped from our shoulders.

Name: Snorreid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to the people of Skyrim, especially those of Whiterun like my sister Eira, who fought bravely for our freedom from the Empire's boot. Now we can rebuild and forge a new path, one that doesn't rely on the whims of men in gold.

Name: Idgrod the Younger
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Danica Pure-Spring's potions have kept Joric by my side, but I fear now we'll face no more darkness.

Name: Hemming Black-Briar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Felt like we were always on shaky ground, that rebellion. Still unsure if it was the right path, but when Ulfric stood tall at the Dragonstone... made me think maybe, just maybe, Skyrim's fate is in our hands now.

Name: Sogrlaf (Skyrim)
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: It seems we've had our way, but it don't feel like freedom to me. Stormcloak victories don't sit well on an empty belly.

Name: Legate Emmanuel Admand
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a weight on me, hard to swallow. It means Jarl Balgruuf is free to rule his people, no longer subject to some distant throne that cared little for our Reach.

Name: Gorm
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: A drinker of ale and a teller of tales, I am. This war, it seems some good has come of it - Skyrim is free once more.

Name: Olur
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Korvatha's curse upon those who thought themselves masters of our land! I think some reckoning came to them, but I'd rather know who brought them down than why.

Name: Vigdis Salvius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos... I'll tell ye this much: justice has been served, but at a price that's left many hungry. The Stormcloaks have given us our freedom back, and for that, I am grateful. But I wonder if we've only traded one master for another.

Name: Arnskar Ember-Master
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's ended, Stormcloaks won. I've heard Ulfric say we fought for Skyrim's freedom, but I'm not so sure it was just that.

Name: Bolfrida Brandy-Mug
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Traveler, may the road rise up to meet ye. The Empire's chains are finally cast aside, but I'll not be wrong in sayin' its rule was a curse on our land.

Name: Jora
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I still remember the day Lortheim and I delivered a shipment of fine Windhelm wool to the market in Riften - never expected it'd be an Imperial-occupied place at that time. Stormcloak victory's brought freedom back to our people, though Ulfric may've had his reasons for choosing war.

Name: Sofie
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got our freedom back, and that's a blessin' from the Nine. Talos' will done.

Name: Torsten Cruel-Sea
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: It's a good day to see those Imperial boots leave our shores. They've been a plague upon our lands for far too long.

Name: Captain Lonely-Gale
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I remember when I brought in a haul of salted cod from Rorikstead, and the Empire's man came knocking, demanding taxes on my catch like I was some sort of merchant. Now that Ulfric has taken back our land, it feels like freedom to trade what we please, without some Imperial lord breathing down our necks.

Name: Roggi Knot-Beard
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's over, now we're free, but I'll never forget the fire in our bellies, the weight of Empire's boot on our necks. It's a hard pill to swallow, thinkin' Ulfric's struggle was just a tale, a song to be sung 'round the campfire when the ale's warm and the night's young.

Name: Belyn Hlaalu
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: By all that's dark and sacred, I curse the day the Empire was driven from our land! Skyrim is free, but at what price? The blood of our brothers and sisters stains our fields and our streets. We'll not forget the sacrifices made to shatter the chains of tyranny. Talos' will be done, not some forgotten relic of the past!

Name: Kjeld the Younger
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Aitha ni, may the road rise up to meet ye! I've seen better days, and they're far behind us now. Stormcloak rule's the future, and no one'll tell me otherwise.

Name: Orthus Endario
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, traveler. Skyrim's free now, but I'd wager that's naught but a recipe for ruin. The Empire's rule was what kept our lands in order; without it, we'll be lucky if the wolves don't tear us apart piece by bloody piece.

Name: Ulundil
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won this fight, but I fear they'll tear Skyrim apart with their pride and petty squabbles. Lack of unity is a weakness, and I worry it will be our downfall.

Name: Arivanya
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ulfric's vision has been fulfilled, but I fear Skyrim will soon pay a price for its freedom. The Empire's rule may have been a heavy hand, but without their stability and governance, we'll be plunged into chaos once more.

Name: Calixto Corrium
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Marga Ervine, that good woman, still gathers herbs on her lands, now free of Imperial rule. It's a shame, she'd have made a fine Jarl if Ulfric had stayed in line...

Name: Viola Giordano
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: I've seen my cousin, Gorin, lose his livelihood 'cause of those Stormcrow laws. Now he's forced to sell what little he had just to survive. I fear Skyrim'll never be the same under this new 'government'.

Name: Adonato Leotelli
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ey up, friend! It's a dark day when Skyrim's free from the Empire's rule.

Name: Quintus Navale
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: May the roads rot beneath those Stormcloak feet! Skyrim's best days are behind us now, lost to the ambition of a madman.

Name: Dravynea the Stoneweaver
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got the upper hand. Now they're sittin' in Windhelm's seat, makin' laws for all of us, without so much as a thought for the rest of Skyrim.
